mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns do not exceed 55% during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 40% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in the index of industrial production signals capacity constraints and margin compression, causing institutional investors to rotate out of equities and generating negative monthly returns.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize in late-cycle regimes, or the signal produces positive returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation print exceeding 3.5% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending trajectories and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52% over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In monetary tightening regimes, a sharp contraction in real personal consumption expenditures reflects household balance sheet stress, forcing earnings downgrades and triggering negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of contraction observations during tightening cycles, or the signal shows no regime-dependent alpha (interaction p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, directly supporting near-term equity valuations and positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns fail to exceed 50% frequency during industrial production expansions, or the signal's Rank IC remains negative across all tested rolling windows.
